title: The political theory of liberal socialism [Book Review]
author: Maiwald

opening_move.description: The review opens by framing McManus's project as a 'retrieval' — a historically-grounded reconstruction of the liberal-socialist tradition for contemporary progressive use.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Recovering and making usable the liberal-socialist tradition for contemporary theoretical and political projects

theoretical_positioning.description: The review is sympathetic and primarily expository, contextualising McManus's synthesis of liberal-socialist thinkers without strong independent critique.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: ideological and intellectual history; close reading of the liberal-socialist canon
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review: the argument is a presentation and modest endorsement of McManus's reconstructive normative project.

case_type.case_name: McManus, The Political Theory of Liberal Socialism (2025)

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. The review does not develop an independent argument but contextualises and endorses McManus's project.
